arxiv_papers.title: Circularity in Perovskite-Based Tandem Photovoltaics for Terawatt-Scale Deployment
arxiv_papers.snippet: As photovoltaics (PVs) scale from one to multiple terawatts over the next decade, ensuring sustainable deployment is urgently required. arxiv_papers.title: Deterministic positioning of circular Bragg gratings using atomic force lithography for high-performance quantum dot light sources
arxiv_papers.snippet: Semiconductor quantum dots (QDs) grown by molecular beam epitaxy are excellent quantum emitters, but their random spatial distribution hinders deterministic coupling to optical microcavities. youtube_items.title: [YT] Recycling Marine Plastic Waste A Circular Economy Case | Suzanna Laskaridis | TEDxAthens
youtube_items.summary: Transcriber: Maria Pericleous Reviewer: Chryssa Rapessi In 2017, I was approached by a team of designers to help finance a project they were doing here in Greece. The aim was to reuse old fishing nets, and turn them into filament for 3D printers. I thought the idea of recycling marine plastic waste into something workable was absolutely fantastic. So I started working in my mind the idea of how this could actually be reused. We put together the best minds in the fields of marine plastic waste, new technologies and circular economy and dove right in. We have a storage area just outside of Athens, so we started suggesting to various friends and NGOs and various other companies who had been collecting marine plastic waste up to that point, that we’d take them back to our storage area.
